betterquesting:10.desc:8:
It was not easy, but it was necessary. The testificates did not take the revelation of the fall of the Arbiter well.
Their god, the god that led them and helped their people from longer than the existence of their home planet.
The benevolent leader that protected them.
The father of a people.
The struggles under the invasion from the Shyre Empire and the Twilight Horde did not hinder their faith and trust in the Arbiter. Their lord was struggling against the Lord of Gluttony, but they would return.
The increasing claims to power from the Meatball Man did not hinder their faith and trust in the Arbiter. The fight was one that continued for ages, after all.
But now, a tomb.
And yet, acceptance came, and with it, recognition of the signs.
I have never seen myself as a leader, and yet, in this, it felt right to come forward, not as an accidental warrior with my own motives, but as their anchor in this time of struggle. To place upon them the touch of the Arbiter.
